notes: Ricky Wysocki FH power_pocket & release: l_hip, l_knee, l_ankle occluded - some angles null
x_factor and hip_rotation identical in 2D side view (both measure shoulder-hip angular separation)
Calvin Heimburg FH: posterior camera view - trunk_tilt may differ from side-view measurements
BH lead/trail knee corrected: RHBH lead=R side, trail=L side

v3.2: Shoulder angle is always flexion (arm elevated from trunk). Removed extension label — in disc golf throwing, the arm does not go below the trunk line. Elbow angle reported as interior angle (180°=straight). Knee angles always reported as interior angle (180°=straight, always flexion).

data_quality_flags.issue: x_factor_deg=163 is extreme outlier (>3 SD from other players)
data_quality_flags.cause: Likely annotation artifact or unusual camera angle - FH throw_dir=right (others are left)
data_quality_flags.recommendation: Review landmark placement on Eagle FH power pocket_2.jpg

data_quality_flags.issue: l_hip, l_knee, l_ankle occluded - 4 angles null
data_quality_flags.cause: Body occlusion in side-view FH footage
data_quality_flags.recommendation: Source alternative clip with visible lead leg, or mark as partial data

data_quality_flags.issue: Posterior camera view (all other FH clips are side view)
data_quality_flags.cause: Only available clip was posterior angle
data_quality_flags.recommendation: trunk_tilt values not directly comparable to side-view measurements
Angles are geometrically valid but landmark R/L mapping may differ

data_quality_flags.issue: FH baselines have high SD across most angles
data_quality_flags.cause: Mixed camera angles (side/posterior) and throw directions, plus smaller effective n for some angles
data_quality_flags.recommendation: Treat FH baselines as rough guides; BH baselines are more reliable

data_quality_flags.cause: 2D side-view cannot disambiguate hip rotation from shoulder-hip separation
data_quality_flags.recommendation: Consider removing hip_rotation from baseline or noting it as redundant with x_factor in 2D

ansur_ii_depth_estimation.method: Foreshortening-based depth estimation using ANSUR II anthropometric ratios
ansur_ii_depth_estimation.principle: Known real-world segment lengths (from stature) compared to observed 2D lengths → rotation angle via acos(observed/known)
ansur_ii_depth_estimation.proportionality_source: Drillis & Contini (1966), validated on ANSUR II male dataset (n=4082)
limitations: Player heights are approximate (not directly measured)
Proportionality constants have low R² for breadths (shoulder R²=0.15, hip R²=0.20) — individual variation is high
Assumes side-view camera is perpendicular to sagittal plane
Does not account for body composition differences (athletes vs military sample)
Segment length ratios assume average proportions — disc golfers may differ
Depth angle estimates compound errors from stature estimate + ratio uncertainty + landmark placement
ansur_ii_depth_estimation.best_use: Relative comparison between phases/players, not absolute 3D reconstruction

angle_conventions.shoulder_flexion_deg: Angle at shoulder (hip-shoulder-elbow). Flexion=arm forward of frontal plane (+), Extension=arm behind (-).
angle_conventions.shoulder_flexion_dir: Phase-determined: BH reach_back/power_pocket/release=flexion, follow_through=extension. FH wind_up/power_pocket=extension, release/follow_through=flexion.
angle_conventions.elbow_angle_deg: Interior angle at elbow (180=straight, decreasing=more flexed).
angle_conventions.lead/trail_knee_flexion_deg: Interior angle at knee (180=straight, decreasing=more flexed). Always reported as flexion.
angle_conventions.note: 2D side-view limitation: shoulder flex/ext direction cannot be determined from geometry alone due to torso rotation. Direction is assigned based on known phase biomechanics.

angles_computed: off_arm_moment_arm_norm (off-arm wrist distance from trunk midline, normalized by trunk length)

power_pocket.visibility_notes: Elbow leading at ~90deg, forearm parallel to ground

release.visibility_notes: Strong wrist snap visible, disc exiting frame

clips.notes: Confirmed FH by Nicholas. Aggressive jump-through style, younger Wysocki. Good side view.
Dedicated slow-motion FH, close-up side view. Much higher quality than Canadian park clip. Deep knee bend and trunk lean signature.

FH.notes: 2 clips. Slow-motion clip is primary reference. Wysocki FH distinguished by deep trunk lean, tucked elbow, highest X-factor in FH group.

wind_up.visibility_notes: Excellent close-up side view. Deep knee bend setup, disc held low.

power_pocket.visibility_notes: Deepest trunk lean of any FH player. Elbow extremely low and tucked. Highest X-factor of FH group. [l_hip, l_knee, l_ankle occluded - some angles null, old estimated values removed]

release.visibility_notes: Full arm extension, disc clearly exiting. Very upright snap at release vs deep lean at power pocket. [l_hip, l_knee, l_ankle occluded - some angles null, old estimated values removed]

follow_through.visibility_notes: High knee lift on lead leg, full hip rotation, arm wraps upward.

anthropometry.source: ANSUR II proportionality constants (Drillis & Contini 1966, validated on ANSUR II male)

clips.notes: Good side view, white shirt, full body
Highest FPS clip in dataset - excellent phase detection

reach_back.visibility_notes: Most elbow flex at reach-back of BH group — longer arm path

power_pocket.visibility_notes: More relaxed elbow than McBeth, wider arc

release.visibility_notes: 240fps clip very useful — slightly less extension than Wysocki

follow_through.visibility_notes: Wide sweeping follow-through, signature Eagle style

clips.notes: First Eagle FH data. Distinctive deep crouch at wind-up, long arm path, explosive hip clearance.

FH.notes: Eagle FH: deepest knee bend at wind-up of any FH player. Long wide arm path. Explosive hip clearance at release.

wind_up.visibility_notes: Deepest knee bend of any FH player — knees nearly at 90deg. Distinctive low crouch.

power_pocket.visibility_notes: Arm extends wider than other FH players at power pocket — longer arc. Rising out of crouch.

release.visibility_notes: Explosive upward hip drive at release, body rising from crouch into throw. Disc exits cleanly.

follow_through.visibility_notes: Wide sweeping arm path continues. Posterior camera angle at end limits precision.

anthropometry.source: ANSUR II proportionality constants (Drillis & Contini 1966, validated on ANSUR II male)
